content: Instead of treating working memory capacity limits as a harmful constraint to eliminate in cutting-edge 2024–2025 active learning agent memory systems, we can reframe these bounds as an built-in active selection mechanism that prioritizes encoding only the highest-uncertainty, curiosity-worthy information for long-term storage. This is analogous to how human working memory capacity limits force us to discard low-information trivialities and prioritize learning surprising, useful knowledge that improves long-term reasoning.
source_topics: agent working memory capacity limits reasoning
